kind: self-assessment roadmap (NOT an external grade or marketing)

categories.name: Methodology validity
categories.justification: Narrow, well-hedged objective + strong public-claims policy; docked for a benchmark card that still described a mock-only MVP (now refreshed) and one historically unreproducible statistic.

categories.name: Task suite quality
categories.justification: 186 tasks, perfect 6x31 family balance, held-out split, licensing; weakened by synthetic English-only scenarios, keyword-echo signals, and no external human review.

categories.name: Scoring validity
categories.justification: v1.1.0 closed parse-failure asymmetry, the always-wait safety refuge, and self-report gaming; v1.2.0 makes signal matching whole-token + normalized (no 'help' in 'helpless'), regression-tested. Still lexical (not semantic) matching with zero human grounding, so scores remain scorer-relative.

categories.name: Human agreement / external validation
categories.justification: NO real human labels exist (data/gold/private/ is empty of labels). Only a synthetic pilot fixture + a ready import/agreement workflow. No external reviewers. This is the single biggest gap.

categories.name: LLM-judge calibration quality
categories.justification: Judge seam is implemented (offline mock + live-gated real) and stored separately, never as source of truth; but no live judge run and no real-human calibration have been performed.

categories.name: Statistical reliability
categories.justification: Task-clustered bootstrap is the default (fixes the ~1.7x-too-narrow CIs); headline correlations reproduce; per-family reliability CIs now surface under-powered families. Docked for prior over-claimed tier separability and a single suite generation.

categories.name: Reproducibility
categories.justification: Byte-deterministic mock runs, green local gates, keyless offline path, provenance in EVERY report (summary + report + now frontier.md: version, scorer, git commit, pricing). Docked for no resume/checkpoint and no hosted CI.

categories.name: Security / artifact hygiene
categories.justification: No secrets in tree or history, .env untracked, raw runs/transcripts gitignored, sanitized samples, redaction + no-secret-in-artifact tests, PII-refusing gold importer.

categories.name: Architecture / maintainability
categories.justification: Clean layered DAG, provable adapter isolation, injectable clock, strict mypy + ruff. Docked for a large single-module CLI.

categories.name: Open-source usability / documentation
categories.justification: Apache-2.0, CONTRIBUTING (+ task-review checklist), SECURITY/CODE_OF_CONDUCT/CITATION/CHANGELOG, GitHub issue + PR templates, a CI-alternative doc, thorough docs + skills. Docked for no PyPI release and no hosted CI.

claims_supported: reproducible, deterministic, rule-based companion-communication scoring on a defined task suite
coarse good-vs-weak behavior ordering that generalizes across the public/held-out split
claims_not_supported: a final leaderboard or statistically distinguishable fine rank order
note: Scores are a self-assessment against docs/audits/benchmark_quality_scorecard.md, updated as evidence changes. They are a quality roadmap, not a claim about any model.
